content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : Raymond Eller, Los Angeles - Letter
of 9/12/78

Date : 10/78

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
Jay Greenwood

Subject:

Answered by phone on 9/29/78 - question was moot
and I provided them with the content of Lee's
Aug. 3, 1978, memo re "late contributions."

78227

FPPC
SEP 15 11 32 AM '78

Sept. 12, 1978


Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
1100 Kay Street
Sacramento, Ca. 95814

Gentlemen or MS's:

If a political committee received contributions which are
not used and ultimately returned to the donors, please
advise your recommended handling on reports under following circumstances:

A. Checks returned immediately

B. Checks returned after ten days, or more.

C. Checks banked routinely, and committee checks
   issued to donors within a few days
                          ten days
                          thirty days

Those points do not appear to be covered in your recordkeeping Guidelines.


Raymond Miller
[signature: Raymond Miller]


344 So. Serrano Ave.
Los Angeles 90036

sections.question:
If a political committee received contributions which are not used and ultimately returned to the donors, please advise your recommended handling on reports under following circumstances:

A. Checks returned immediately

B. Checks returned after ten Days, or more.

C. Checks banked routinely, and committee checks issued to donors within a few days
    ten days
    thirty days

These points do not appear to be covered in your record-keeping guidelines.
sections.question_synthetic: What is the proper reporting treatment for political committee contributions that are received but not used and subsequently returned to donors under various timing scenarios (immediate return, delayed return, or banking then issuing refund checks)?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC answered by phone on 9/29/78, determining the question was moot and provided reference to Lee's August 3, 1978 memo regarding late contributions as guidance for handling.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a hybrid document containing both the incoming request letter and a memo indicating FPPC's telephonic response. No formal written conclusion section exists; the response was delivered by phone on 9/29/78. The document lacks traditional Q&A section headers but includes the question text in letter form. Confidence reduced due to lack of explicit formal conclusion/advice in writing.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: If a political committee received contributions which are not used and ultimately returned to the donors, please advise your recommended handling on reports under following circumstances:

A. Checks returned immediately

B. Checks returned after ten Days, or more.

C. Checks banked routinely, and committee checks issued to donors within a few days
    ten days
    thirty days

These points do not appear to be covered in your record-keeping guidelines.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC answered by phone on 9/29/78, determining the question was moot and provided reference to Lee's August 3, 1978 memo regarding late contributions as guidance for handling.

Checks returned immediately B. Checks returned after ten Days, or more. C. embedding.summary: Requestor Raymond Eller inquired about the proper reporting treatment for returned political contributions under various circumstances. The FPPC responded telephonically, determining the question was moot and referred the requestor to existing guidance on late contributions.
